reforms.description: A reform package for 2024-2028 was presented to invest EUR 14 billion for health and long-term care, focusing on digitalisation of health services and strengthening the ambulatory sector.
reforms.package_name: 2024 Health and Long-term Care Reform Package
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in health services and digitalisation can enhance productivity and improve health outcomes, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: A reform package for 2024-2028 was presented in November 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in health infrastructure and services, which is crucial for addressing long-term care challenges and improving public health.
reforms.source_quote: A reform package for 2024-2028 was presented in November 2023 to invest EUR 14 billion for health and long-term care.

reforms.description: Austria has developed a 'Walking Master Plan for 2030' aimed at increasing the share of trips taken by foot from 17% in 2020 to 20% by 2030, which includes subsidies for pedestrian infrastructure and mandatory local masterplans for large cities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging walking infrastructure investments can enhance urban mobility and reduce emissions, contributing to overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Walking Master Plan has been developed and is set to be implemented by 2030.
reforms.importance_rationale: The plan represents a significant initiative to improve urban infrastructure for walking, which can have lasting impacts on urban mobility and environmental sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: Austria has developed a 'Walking Master Plan for 2030' targeting a 20% share of trips taken by foot.

reforms.description: The 'Cycling Masterplan' aims to increase the share of cycling in the modal split from 8% in 2020 to 13% by 2030, proposing investments in bicycle infrastructure and awareness-raising measures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investments in cycling infrastructure can promote sustainable transport options, reduce congestion, and enhance urban livability, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Cycling Masterplan has been proposed with specific targets for 2030.
reforms.importance_rationale: This plan represents a significant commitment to enhancing cycling infrastructure, which can lead to long-term benefits for urban transport and environmental sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: The Cycling Masterplan targets an increase in the share of cycling in the modal split to 13% by 2030.

reforms.description: Austria is expanding its support for innovation activities by enhancing fiscal incentives, particularly focusing on increasing equity financing options for startups and innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing equity financing will facilitate access to capital for innovative firms, thereby boosting their growth potential and overall economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to enhance fiscal incentives for innovation activities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the approach to financing innovation, aiming to address the low take-up of risk capital in Austria.
reforms.source_quote: Innovation should be further encouraged, including through the expansion of equity financing.

reforms.description: Austria's 'Broadband Austria 2030' strategy aims to enhance digital infrastructure by investing EUR 1.4 billion until 2026 to expand communications infrastructure in low-density areas, alongside a EUR 900 million allocation from the Recovery and Resilience Plan for gigabit-capable networks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving digital infrastructure is expected to enhance productivity and innovation by facilitating better access to high-speed internet, particularly in underserved areas.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy includes significant public investments and is part of the Recovery and Resilience Plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a substantial investment in national infrastructure that is critical for enhancing digital connectivity and supporting future innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Austria’s 2030 broadband strategy, ‘Broadband Austria 2030’, plans for public investments in areas of low density and higher incentives for private investment.

reforms.description: Austria introduced the Flexible Company - 'Flexible Kapitalgesellschaft' as a new legal form of corporation specifically designed to meet the needs of startups, facilitating the establishment of new companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform aims to lower barriers for startups, potentially increasing entrepreneurial activity and innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The Flexible Company was introduced as part of the startup package reform.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the legal framework for startups, which can enhance the business environment for new ventures.
reforms.source_quote: In 2024, Austria introduced the Flexible Company - 'Flexible Kapitalgesellschaft' as a new legal form of corporation specially oriented to the needs of startups.

reforms.description: Austria's government provides substantial tax and spending incentives for the private sector to innovate, including a research premium that reimburses 14% of firms' entire research expenditures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing R&D spending, this policy encourages firms to invest in innovation, which can lead to productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government provides substantial tax and spending incentives for the private sector to innovate.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a major component of Austria's innovation strategy, significantly impacting R&D investment across the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: The government provides substantial tax and spending incentives for the private sector to innovate, including the research premium - a tax credit as reimbursement for 14% of firms’ entire research expenditures.

reforms.description: In June 2023, Austria established a new state-financed venture capital fund, the aws Gründungsfonds II, aimed at investing in young, innovative companies during their startup and growth phases. The fund is designed to leverage private investments, with a total target of EUR 500 million, although the initial public funding of EUR 72 million is relatively modest compared to similar initiatives in other countries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The fund aims to stimulate private investment in innovative startups, enhancing the overall startup ecosystem and fostering economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund was established and is operational as of June 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the fund represents a significant step towards enhancing the startup ecosystem in Austria, although the scale of funding is smaller compared to similar initiatives in other OECD countries.
reforms.source_quote: In June 2023 a new state-financed venture capital fund (aws Gründungsfonds II: 'Startup Fund') was established with a duration of min. 10 years.

reforms.description: Austria's innovation support is shifting towards societal and mission-oriented targets, which are defined as coordinated policy measures aimed at mobilizing science, technology, and innovation to address specific societal challenges. This approach aims to enhance the strategic orientation of research and innovation interventions, particularly in areas like climate change and AI.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning R&D funding with societal challenges, the reform is expected to enhance the relevance and impact of research, driving innovation that addresses pressing issues.
reforms.status_evidence: The support for innovation in Austria could also shift towards societal and mission-oriented targets.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategic shift represents a significant change in how innovation funding is directed, focusing on societal challenges, which could lead to more impactful research outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: The support for innovation in Austria could also shift towards societal and mission-oriented targets.

reforms.description: Austria is planning a significant effort on innovation, earmarking EUR 330 million for four missions focused on the green transformation, including energy transition and climate-neutral cities, as part of its updated climate plan.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment in green technologies and innovation is expected to enhance productivity and support the transition to a sustainable economy.
reforms.status_evidence: Significant funds are earmarked for innovation over the next three years.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a substantial commitment to innovation in key sectors, aligning with national climate goals and addressing urgent environmental challenges.
reforms.source_quote: Austria is planning a significant effort on innovation including in international collaborative projects.

reforms.description: The government plans to increase research funding from the Ministry of Finance by 5% in 2024, aiming to enhance support for green technologies.
reforms.component_name: Increased R&D Funding for Green Technologies
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing funding for R&D in green technologies is expected to foster innovation and contribute to environmental sustainability.
reforms.status_evidence: Research funding from the Ministry of Finance is scheduled to increase by 5% in 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: This increase in funding is a significant but narrower adjustment aimed at enhancing support for specific sectors, particularly green technologies.
reforms.source_quote: Additional effort is scheduled, as research funding from the Ministry of Finance is scheduled to increase by 5% in 2024.

reforms.description: Austria introduced the KlimaTicket, a nationwide public transport ticket that allows unlimited travel across the country, aimed at promoting the use of public transport and reducing reliance on cars.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages public transport use, which can reduce traffic congestion and emissions, contributing to a more sustainable economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The KlimaTicket was launched and has gained a significant number of subscribers.
reforms.importance_rationale: The KlimaTicket represents a significant initiative to promote sustainable transport, with potential long-term impacts on commuting patterns and urban mobility.
reforms.source_quote: In October 2021, Austria introduced a nationwide climate ticket – the 'KlimaTicket', which offers almost unlimited public transport use across the country.

reforms.description: Austria has implemented a funding program (LADIN) to support the deployment of charging stations in underserved areas, with an initial amount of EUR 7 million for the first tender, addressing rural access to electric vehicle charging infrastructure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improves access to charging infrastructure, facilitating the adoption of electric vehicles and supporting the transition to a low-carbon economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government started a funding program for the deployment of charging stations in underserved areas.
reforms.importance_rationale: Addresses a critical barrier to electric vehicle adoption in rural areas, enhancing the overall infrastructure for sustainable transport.
reforms.source_quote: The government started a funding program (LADIN) for the deployment of charging stations in underserved areas with an initial amount of EUR 7 million for the first tender.

reforms.description: Austria has introduced a credit mechanism allowing the electricity used to charge electric vehicles at public or private charging stations to be sold as carbon credits, incentivizing the use of renewable energy for EV charging.
reforms.component_name: Carbon Credit Mechanism for EV Charging
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages the use of renewable energy in electric vehicle charging, promoting sustainability and innovation in the energy sector.
reforms.status_evidence: Austria has implemented a credit mechanism whereby the electricity used to charge EVs can be sold as carbon credits.
reforms.importance_rationale: This mechanism enhances the economic viability of renewable energy use in electric vehicle charging, contributing to broader climate goals.
reforms.source_quote: Austria is also one of the four European countries which has implemented (starting in 2023) a credit mechanism whereby the electricity used to charge EVs at public or private charging stations can be sold to intermediaries as carbon credits.

reforms.description: Austria is developing a National Infrastructure Plan (NIP) as part of the Renewables Expansion Act to address the barriers to grid expansion and ensure the necessary energy infrastructure for the transformation of the energy sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The NIP aims to streamline grid access and development, facilitating the deployment of renewable energy sources, which is essential for economic growth and energy transition.
reforms.status_evidence: A draft NIP has been sent for public consultation during the summer of 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: The NIP represents a structural change in Austria's approach to energy infrastructure, addressing critical barriers to renewable energy deployment and aligning with long-term energy goals.
reforms.source_quote: The creation of an integrated Austrian network infrastructure plan (NIP) as part of the Renewables Expansion Act is thus highly relevant.

reforms.description: Austria is proposing a new Act to create a green hydrogen production support scheme with funds of EUR 400 million to promote the development of renewable hydrogen as part of its energy strategy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This scheme aims to enhance the production of renewable hydrogen, which is crucial for decarbonising various sectors and supporting Austria's transition to a sustainable energy system.
reforms.status_evidence: A new Act creating a green hydrogen production support scheme with funds of EUR 400 million has been under public consultation since February 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a dedicated support scheme for green hydrogen production represents a significant policy shift towards sustainable energy solutions, with substantial funding allocated to drive innovation in this sector.
reforms.source_quote: A new Act creating a green hydrogen production support scheme with funds of EUR 400 million has been under public consultation since February 2024.

reforms.description: Austria is reviewing its ban on the development of permanent geological storage of carbon dioxide, which has been in place since 2011. This review is part of a broader strategy to enhance carbon capture and storage technologies, which are crucial for decarbonising the steel and cement sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating carbon storage is expected to enhance the development of green technologies, thereby supporting innovation in the industrial sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The review of the ban is part of the Climate Ministry's commissioned feasibility study.
reforms.importance_rationale: The review represents a significant policy shift that could enable the deployment of critical technologies for emissions reduction, impacting the industrial sector's innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Austria has banned projects to develop permanent geological storage of carbon dioxide since 2011... The legally-scheduled evaluation of the ban every five years... should provide the chance to revive discussion on the potential for storage in Austria.

reforms.description: The Austrian government has allocated EUR 2.9 billion to fund projects that significantly reduce emissions in industry, with a focus on innovative technologies at the prototype stage. This funding aims to spur innovation and support the transition to greener industrial processes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing substantial funding for innovative projects, the initiative is expected to enhance technological development and reduce emissions, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has allocated EUR 2.9 billion to provide grants through tenders to fund projects which provide significant emissions reductions.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding represents a major investment in innovation aimed at reducing industrial emissions, which is critical for Austria's climate goals and economic transformation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has allocated EUR 2.9 billion to provide grants through tenders to fund projects which provide significant emissions reductions in industry.

reforms.description: Austria has introduced targeted subsidies for the replacement of fossil-fuel heating systems and the renovation of old buildings, aiming to support vulnerable households while addressing energy efficiency.
reforms.component_name: Targeted Subsidies for Heating and Renovation
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The subsidies are designed to reduce energy consumption and emissions, thereby promoting energy efficiency and supporting the transition to greener technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced the introduction of targeted subsidies for heating system replacements and building renovations.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in Austria's approach to energy efficiency and climate policy, with substantial budget allocations aimed at transforming the building sector.
reforms.source_quote: Austria has recently complemented its universal subsidy schemes with targeted support for vulnerable households.
